intro: Wandering aimlessly through the sea, you’ve effectively gotten nowhere.
title: Lost at Sea
description:
The sea is turbulent in this area, and you’re running out of energy.
This is bad. Is there a way out of the situation?

1.label: {{option1PrimaryName}} Might Help
1.tooltipDisabled: You have no Pokémon to {{option1RequiredMove}} on
1.selected: {{option1PrimaryName}} swims ahead, guiding you back on track.${{option1PrimaryName}} seems to also have gotten stronger in this time of need!

2.label: {{option2PrimaryName}} Might Help
2.tooltipDisabled: You have no Pokémon to {{option2RequiredMove}} with
2.selected: {{option2PrimaryName}} flies ahead of your boat, guiding you back on track.${{option2PrimaryName}} seems to also have gotten stronger in this time of need!

3.label: Wander Aimlessly
3.tooltip: (-) Each of your Pokémon lose {{damagePercentage}}% of their total HP
3.selected: You float about in the boat, steering without direction until you finally spot a landmark you remember.$You and your Pokémon are fatigued from the whole ordeal.

4.label: Let {{option4PrimaryName}} Swim
4.tooltip:
(?) {{option4PrimaryName}} may find a strange current
(+) {{option4PrimaryName}} gains some EXP
4.tooltipDisabled: You need Totodile, Croconaw, or Feraligatr in fighting shape
4.selected: {{option4PrimaryName}} leaps into the water with a delighted bark and grabs the rope in its jaws.$For a while, this seems like a fantastic idea.$Then the boat begins moving faster. Then much faster.$By the time you manage to get {{option4PrimaryName}}'s attention, the familiar coastline is long gone.$You drift through narrow canals, under stone bridges, and into a sunlit city built over the water.${{option4PrimaryName}} looks extremely proud of itself.
4.rejected: {{option4PrimaryName}} paddles toward a strange current, but the group cannot agree on following it.$The boat drifts aimlessly until you finally spot a familiar landmark.
